name: Erretzu MTB Loop
description: This difficult 11.3-mile mountain biking loop around Erretzü summit offers panoramic Pyrenees views and technical singletrack.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Trois-Villes are set within a varied natural landscape in the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department of southwest France. The commune features elevations ranging from 195 meters to 793 meters above sea level, offering diverse terrain for cycling. Situated near the Pyrenees mountains, the area provides a tranquil environment with a mix of ascents and descents. This region's natural surroundings contribute to a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Trois-Villes The most popular mountain bike trail is Erretzu MTB Loop, a 11.3 miles (18.2 km) difficult route that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tardets-Soholus – Tardets loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense, a moderate 11.6 miles (18.6 km) path. This route offers a balanced mix of terrain suitable for intermediate riders. Local mountain bikers also love the Tardets-Soholus – Abense de Haut Bridge loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense, a 20.1 miles (32.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Mountain biking around Trois-Villes is defined by varied elevations, natural settings, and proximity to the Pyrenees mountains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Trois-Villes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Trois-Villes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Trois-Villes?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Trois-Villes. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, with options for various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking trails in Trois-Villes?
A: The trails around Trois-Villes cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 25 moderate routes, and 31 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Trois-Villes?
A: The mountain biking trails around Trois-Villes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied natural landscapes, the tranquility of the area, and the diverse terrain that offers both ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Trois-Villes?
A: Yes, there are a couple of easy mountain bike trails around Trois-Villes that could be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and less technical terrain, allowing for a more relaxed experience in the natural surroundings.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Trois-Villes?
A: Mountain bike trails in Trois-Villes feature varied terrain due to elevations ranging from 195 meters to 793 meters above sea level. You can expect a mix of ascents and descents, natural paths, and diverse landscapes characteristic of the Pyrénées-Atlantiques department.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Trois-Villes?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Trois-Villes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963755141/erretzu-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erretzu MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24570435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets-Soholus – Tardets loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate circular option.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you might encounter several interesting points. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/797632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorama 360°&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views, or explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3511274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along the Gave de Larrau&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3368769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madeleine Chapel&lt;/a&gt; is another point of interest in the vicinity.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Trois-Villes?
A: Given the tranquil natural setting and proximity to the Pyrenees, spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Trois-Villes. The weather is typically milder, making for enjoyable rides through the varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking longer challenges, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37578731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Madeleine – Ruig pad loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt; cover over 37 kilometers with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass through towns or villages?
A: Some routes may pass near or through local communities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24570435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets-Soholus – Tardets loop from Alos-Sibas-Abense&lt;/a&gt; connects areas around Tardets, offering a chance to experience the local atmosphere. The highlight &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6723006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardets&lt;/a&gt; itself is a small town you might encounter.

Q: Are there any specific trails known for their scenic beauty?
A: Many trails in Trois-Villes offer scenic beauty due to the region's varied natural landscapes and proximity to the Pyrenees. Routes that feature significant elevation changes often provide rewarding views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/797632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panorama 360°&lt;/a&gt; highlight suggests areas with expansive vistas.
tips: Demanding climb to reach the top of Erretzu (700m altitude). Wonderful landscape !

faqs.question: Is the Montée exigeante au sommet d'Erretzu mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is categorized as difficult and is not recommended for beginners. It requires good physical condition and excellent piloting skills due to its technical sections and challenging terrain. Some parts may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The trail largely consists of technical singletrack paths, with a majority of unpaved surfaces. You can expect rocky climbs that demand explosiveness and pedaling flexibility, as well as continuous sequences of ascents and descents. After an initial gradual climb, the route transitions into these more technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied and beautiful landscapes, including all-around panoramic views of the Pyrenees. You'll pass through fern-covered hillsides that turn a flamboyant red in autumn, sections of undergrowth alongside a picturesque stream, and areas dotted with pollarded oak trees. The south face of the massif often glows with a reddish hue.

faqs.question: Is the Montée exigeante au sommet d'Erretzu a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this mountain biking route is designed as a comprehensive loop around the Erretzü summit, offering a diverse and continuous riding experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can often be ridden year-round due to its quick-drying nature. However, autumn is particularly scenic as the fern-covered hillsides transform into a vibrant red, adding to the beauty of the ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Montée exigeante au sommet d'Erretzu trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, the trail is a popular spot for locals from Tardets. It's advisable to look for public parking areas in or near Tardets, which would provide convenient access to the general area where the trail begins.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Montée exigeante au sommet d'Erretzu trail?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify restrictions regarding dogs on this trail. However, as a general rule for mountain biking trails in natural areas, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash to ensure their safety and respect wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access the Montée exigeante au sommet d'Erretzu trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are electric mountain bikes (e-bikes) recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: No, the use of electric mountain bikes (VAE) is not recommended on this trail. Its challenging nature, technical sections, and rocky climbs are best suited for traditional mountain bikes and riders with excellent piloting skills.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as Boucle de la Madeleine, Boucle de Camou, and Le tour de Soule, among others like La grotte des Laminak and Errege bidea.

faqs.question: What makes this trail particularly challenging for mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: The challenge comes from a continuous sequence of climbs and descents, combined with technical singletrack sections and rocky ascents. It demands both physical endurance and advanced bike handling skills, making it a rewarding experience for sports enthusiasts.
